goal: Import a SolidWorks STEP end-of-arm tool into Isaac Sim with mm units preserved and return a drawing-grade clearance number against a UR10e in stow.
thoughts: STEP must be converted through the CAD Importer to USD -- confirm units land as mm on the stage (Isaac Sim stores in meters internally, so metersPerUnit=0.001). Place EOAT relative to UR10e flange via get_world_transform. Report nearest-approach with a tolerance note; flag that threaded holes typically become plain cylinders in STEP->USD so thread clearance is not a real answer.
code:
eoat = import_template(
    template_name="step_import",
    source_path="<HOME>/CAD/eoat_vgrip_v4.step",
    target_path="/World/UR10e/Flange/EOAT",
    units="mm",
    metersPerUnit=0.001
)
add_reference(prim_path="/World/UR10e/Flange/EOAT", reference_path=eoat.usd_path)
flange_xf = get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/UR10e/tool0")
# Use flange_xf to align EOAT base
collisions = check_collisions(
    subjects=["/World/UR10e/Flange/EOAT"],
    targets=["/World/UR10e"],
    ignore_self=True
)
clearance = check_path_clearance(
    prim_path="/World/UR10e/Flange/EOAT",
    waypoints=["<STOW_POSE>"],
    others=["/World/UR10e/base_link", "/World/UR10e/shoulder_link"],
    units="mm"
)
dist = measure_distance(
    prim_a="/World/UR10e/Flange/EOAT/tip",
    prim_b="/World/UR10e/shoulder_link",
    units="mm"
)
failure_modes: Losing mm units through STEP->USD conversion and reporting in meters silently
Claiming thread clearance is valid when STEP->USD collapses threads to cylinders
Giving a single-decimal clearance number without a tolerance / mesh-fidelity caveat
